skill.name: sprint-retrospective
skill.description: Facilitate effective sprint retrospectives for continuous team improvement. Use when conducting team retrospectives, identifying improvements, or fostering team collaboration. Handles retrospective formats, action items, and facilitation techniques.
skill.summary: Guide agile teams through structured retrospectives with clear action items.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged backticks, weak-crypto keywords, network reconnaissance, and hardcoded URLs. Review found Markdown templates, inline tags, normal prose, and visible reference links, with no executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or command invocation. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from external reference links.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited content is Markdown code fences and inline topic tags. No command execution syntax or instruction to run shell commands is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown delimiters and inline tags in documentation. There is no script file, interpreter directive, shell command, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Keyword-Based Blocker Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and network reconnaissance blockers. The cited lines contain normal descriptive words and retrospective examples, not cryptographic code, scanners, ports, hosts, or network discovery behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plain-language facilitation content and action-item examples. No evidence of cryptographic implementation or network reconnaissance functionality was found.

low_findings.title: External Reference Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes two visible third-party reference links. They are not fetched by code and do not transmit data, but users should evaluate external destinations before opening them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs are present, but only as Markdown references. This is a minor marketplace-content concern, not runtime network behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams often leave retrospectives with vague feedback and weak follow-up. This skill structures formats, facilitation prompts, and action items for clearer improvement cycles.
actual_capabilities: Provides Start-Stop-Continue, Mad-Sad-Glad, and 4Ls retrospective formats.
Structures team feedback into themed retrospective sections.
Creates specific action items with owners and follow-up expectations.
Defines facilitation rules for safe, blame-free discussion.
Suggests concise best practices for time-boxing and rotating facilitators.
limitations: Does not collect anonymous feedback or manage live voting by itself.
Does not integrate directly with Jira, Linear, Slack, or calendar tools.
Requires the user to provide team context, sprint outcomes, and discussion notes.
Cannot enforce completion of action items after the retrospective.

use_cases.title: Sprint Closeout
use_cases.description: Convert sprint notes into a balanced retrospective with clear action items.

use_cases.title: Process Improvement
use_cases.description: Identify recurring delivery problems and turn them into practical experiments.

use_cases.title: Team Health Check
use_cases.description: Facilitate a structured conversation after a milestone or team conflict.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Retrospective
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sprint-retrospective skill to create a Start-Stop-Continue retrospective for Sprint 12. Include sections and three action items.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sprint-retrospective skill to recommend a retrospective format for a team with changing requirements, test instability, and low review time.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Notes into Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sprint-retrospective skill to organize these notes into themes and create two or three specific action items with owners.

prompt_templates.title: Facilitate a Sensitive Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sprint-retrospective skill to plan a blame-free retrospective after a missed release. Include format, facilitation rules, and follow-up structure.

output_examples.input: A team completed all planned work but had late design changes and weak documentation.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a retrospective with wins, pain points, and two focused actions: add design pre-review and reserve documentation time.

output_examples.input: A team wants a format for discussing frustration, disappointment, and positive outcomes.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends Mad-Sad-Glad and organizes comments into emotional themes with practical improvement steps.

output_examples.input: A team needs a milestone retrospective after adopting a new stack.
output_examples.output: The skill uses 4Ls to capture what the team liked, learned, lacked, and wanted for the next delivery cycle.

best_practices: Provide sprint facts, team context, and known incidents before asking for output.
Limit final action items to two or three items with owners and due dates.
Review previous action items before starting the next retrospective.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to assign personal blame or rank individual performance.
Do not request a long list of actions that the team cannot realistically complete.
Do not skip team discussion and treat the generated document as the final retro outcome.

faq.question: Which retrospective formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers Start-Stop-Continue, Mad-Sad-Glad, and 4Ls formats.

faq.question: Can it create action items?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps turn observations into specific action items with owners and follow-up expectations.

faq.question: Does it run live retrospective meetings?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and content, but the user still facilitates the meeting.

faq.question: Can it work with incomplete sprint notes?
faq.answer: Yes, but better notes produce more useful themes and actions.

faq.question: Is it only for Scrum teams?
faq.answer: No. Any product or delivery team can use the formats after a cycle or milestone.

faq.question: Does it connect to project management tools?
faq.answer: No. It does not directly integrate with ticketing, calendar, or chat systems.
